samples.xml_description:
Implement an 8-bit multiplier based on shifting and adding. 8-bit input operand A (multiplicand). 8-bit input operand B (multiplier). 16-bit output product. 8 3

Algorithm:

  1. Initialize
  2. Iterate i from 0 to b - 1.
  3. If the ith bit of B is set (if (B[i])), add A to the product at the position i (i.e., product[i] += A).

    Note:

    • The addition is meant to be carried out using a simple assignment for the sake of clarity, not bitwise addition with a carry-out.
    • The addition is carried out at the ith bit of the multiplier, not at the ith stage.
  4. Shift A left by one position (logical shift).

Example:

Example inputs: A = 0000 0010 (2) B = 0000 0011 (3) Initialization: product = 0000 0000 0000 0000 (0) Iteration 1 (i = 0): B[0] = 1, so add A to the product at position 0:
product = 0000 0000 0000 0010 (2) Iteration 2 (i = 1): B[1] = 1, so add A to the product at position 1:
product = 0000 0000 0000 0000 + 0000 0000 0000 0100 (4) = 0000 0000 0000 0100 (4) Iteration 3 (i = 2): B[2] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 2:
product = 0000 0000 0000 0100 (4) Iteration 4 (i = 3): B[3] = 1, so add A to the product at position 3:
product = 0000 0000 0000 0100 (4) + 0000 0000 0010 0000 (64) = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68) Shift A left by one position: A = 0000 0100 (4) << 1 = 0000 1000 (8) Iteration 5 (i = 4): B[4] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 4:
product = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68) Iteration 6 (i = 5): B[5] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 5:
product = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68) Iteration 7 (i = 6): B[6] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 6:
product = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68) Iteration 8 (i = 7): B[7] = 0, so do not add A to the product at position 7:
product = 0000 0000 0010 0100 (68)

Note:

  • The addition is carried out at the ith bit of the multiplier, not at the ith stage.
  • The multiplication algorithm is designed to prevent the addition from being carried out at the ith stage.
